_about: Per-script context for the vault dashboard: a Chiron-vocabulary title and what each module does mathematically, programmatically, and conceptually. Scripts not listed here fall back to their docstring purpose.

chiron.title: The Invariant Engine
chiron.math: argmin over typed generator classes of the two-part MDL code L(M)+L(s|M) in exact rationals, accepted only if it predicts withheld terms at exact equality; otherwise INCOMPRESSIBLE.
chiron.prog: one self-contained file: collapse()/articulate() codec, owner-signed certificates, an offline HTTP console; no third-party deps in the core path.
chiron.concept: recover the exact rule beneath a codified surface, prove it on data it never saw, or honestly refuse — never a confident guess.

semic.title: The Semantic Invariant Calculus
semic.math: edge-set energy E(S)=α|S|+Σ|Oᵢ △ S| is separable per edge, giving the closed-form argmin S*={e : count(e) > (n+α)/2} in O(N·m); the MDL collapse equals the T→0 limit of the Gibbs sampler.
semic.prog: pure-stdlib; surface_skeleton → collapse → certify; typed classes H1–H5; exhaustive/separable/branch-and-bound solvers agree; 56/56 gates.
semic.concept: the recovery discipline lifted from integers to meaning: find the shared invariant of a family of texts, verify on held-out members, refuse a contaminated pool.

semic_energy.title: The Energy Stack
semic_energy.math: three levels — exact collapse (L1), the constraint manifold (L2), and a Gibbs measure p_T(S)∝exp(−E(S)/T) ranking (L3); landscape entropy separates a flat (ambiguous) from a peaked (decisive) basin.
semic_energy.prog: builds only on semic's exact core; three_level() returns CERTIFIED or, on refusal, explicitly UNCERTIFIED energy-ranked candidates — it never certifies at L3.
semic_energy.concept: stay exact wherever possible; only when exact recovery refuses do you explore approximations, always labelled uncertified.

epistemic.title: The Epistemic Contract
epistemic.math: the schema Surface → Hypothesis space → Constraint space → Verification → Certificate, with four engines proven to be instances of one pipeline.
epistemic.prog: a RecoveryEngine interface plus Chiron / semic / governance / energy adapters driven uniformly.
epistemic.concept: the repository is not many projects — it is one contract instantiated across integers, meaning, law, and probability.

density_emotion.title: The Decoherence Channel
density_emotion.math: competing readings as a density matrix evolved by a Lindblad CPTP semigroup with a deterministic half-life; von Neumann entropy decays as interpretations decohere toward one.
density_emotion.prog: complete-positivity and trace preservation asserted at every step; deterministic; emits a signed certificate; 8/8 selftest.
density_emotion.concept: model ambiguity as physics — rival interpretations interfere and settle, with a provable, honest collapse rather than a hand-waved one.

certify_finding.title: The Certificate
certify_finding.math: scores a finding against the Daubert / FRE-702 factors and binds the evidence under a Merkle root with a replay seed.
certify_finding.prog: emits a structured, reproducible attestation carrying an explicit, honest legal_status.
certify_finding.concept: turn a recovered fact into a court-ready, replayable record — an input a human decision can stand on, never the decision itself.

govern.title: The Governance Gate
govern.math: the SoCPM decision rule over a Truth-Horizon partition (Knowns / Unknowns / void) against a fixed admissibility threshold.
govern.prog: a LexGuard gate applied to any claim; admit, or escalate to a human.
govern.concept: a standard of care for persuasive machines — a claim is allowed only if it clears the bar, otherwise a person decides.

legal_corpus.title: The Codified Law
legal_corpus.math: a provision set with domain scoping; applicable(domain) ∩ keyword retrieval recovers the governing rules.
legal_corpus.prog: a hardcoded corpus of statutes, regulations, treaties, and orders (EU AI Act, GDPR, NIST AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001).
legal_corpus.concept: the explicit law the governance layer reasons over — stated in full, not implied.

llm_certify.title: The Accountability Wrapper
llm_certify.math: extracts the checkable claims from free text (arithmetic and numeric sequences via collapse) and reports how many of M verify exactly, under a Merkle root.
llm_certify.prog: wraps any language-model output: candor audit, exact verification, governance gate; the verdict is modest by construction.
llm_certify.concept: the model proposes, the engine disposes — certify what is checkable, and never call free text 'correct.'

llm_providers.title: The Proposer Chain
llm_providers.math: a fallback ordering over providers returning the first non-empty answer, plus a fan-out that gathers every keyed provider's proposal for the verifier to test.
llm_providers.prog: an env-keyed registry (gemini, openrouter, groq, openai, anthropic, perplexity; llama/qwen via OpenRouter), mock-testable, keys read only from the environment.
llm_providers.concept: many possible proposers, one exact verifier — the proposer can be anyone; the source of truth is never the model.

chiron_artifact.title: The Memorial
chiron_artifact.math: a dual-view certificate (machine evidence + human claim) admitted only if it carries a non-empty falsifier.
chiron_artifact.prog: quick() emits a signed record to artifacts/<script>/; refuses any claim with no stated way to break it.
chiron_artifact.concept: every run can leave proof of what it found, why it is believed, and the one thing that would falsify it.

build_manifest.title: The Index
build_manifest.math: a per-script feature record — runnable, self-testing, stdlib-only, header-stamped, certificate-emitting, last result.
build_manifest.prog: walks the spine, runs each selftest (positional or --selftest), and writes manifest.json.
build_manifest.concept: the catalog that makes the whole vault auditable at a glance.

chiron_ciphers.title: The Cipher Solver
chiron_ciphers.math: recovers a classical transform and key by inverting it under the engine's language model; a known pair recovers the key directly.
chiron_ciphers.prog: Caesar / affine / substitution / transposition / base-n / Morse, ciphertext-only; seeds a cryptography basis.
chiron_ciphers.concept: decoding is just inverse recovery — the same collapse, run backwards through a transform.

intake.title: The Front Door
intake.math: normalizes arbitrary input (JSON / numeric / text) into the typed surfaces the engine accepts.
intake.prog: a strict structured-intake parser feeding collapse.
intake.concept: anything codified can enter — once it is a surface, the same law applies.

intake_salvage.title: The Salvage Door
intake_salvage.math: a fault-tolerant parse that never overwrites a good interpretation with a worse one.
intake_salvage.prog: wraps strict intake so malformed input degrades gracefully instead of failing.
intake_salvage.concept: uncooperative material still gets read — the best available structure survives.

language.title: Offline Language
language.math: n-gram statistics with PPMI semantics — no model weights, no network.
language.prog: builds a prose brief and a likelihood signal entirely in stdlib.
language.concept: the engine can read and weigh natural language without depending on any external model.

discernment.title: Independent Witnesses
discernment.math: confidence by convergence of independent sources rather than by any single estimate.
discernment.concept: belief earned by corroboration — multiple paths to the same answer, not one loud one.

ontological.title: Value & Harmony
ontological.math: V-Units and a Harmony Functional scoring coherence across a structure.
ontological.prog: a deterministic value score over a recovered object.
ontological.concept: ask not only what a structure is, but how well its parts cohere.

actionable_intelligence.title: The Decision Brief
actionable_intelligence.math: combines an exact forecast, an exact anomaly flag, and a governed recommendation.
actionable_intelligence.prog: assembles a decision-ready brief from the engine's verified outputs.
actionable_intelligence.concept: intelligence that is not just true but usable — and cleared by governance before it is offered.

operator_calculus.title: The Six Operators
operator_calculus.math: six epistemic operators acting on a Mystery Vector ξ ∈ ℝ⁶ (six species of the unknown).
operator_calculus.prog: a small algebra over a typed uncertainty state.
operator_calculus.concept: a vocabulary for the kinds of not-knowing, so each can be handled on its own terms.

projection_stack.title: The Projection Stack
projection_stack.math: projects a finding through P→C→E→S with identity preserved as a conservation law.
projection_stack.prog: a layered transform that carries provenance through each stage.
projection_stack.concept: a result can change form without losing what it is — identity persists under projection.

significance.title: Curvature as Significance
significance.math: Ollivier–Ricci curvature over the structure locates where meaning concentrates; W₁ the underlying transport.
significance.prog: computes a curvature field and reports its peaks.
significance.concept: significance is geometric — it is where the structure bends most sharply.

holographic.title: Boundary Recovery
holographic.math: reconstructs interior structure from an erased boundary fragment (holographic error-correction analogy).
holographic.prog: recovers a whole from a partial edge.
holographic.concept: the rule is often legible from a fragment — the boundary already encodes the bulk.

aesthetics.title: Mathematical Beauty
aesthetics.math: the score B = (E·S·R)^{1/3} over elegance, symmetry, and resonance.
aesthetics.prog: a deterministic beauty metric over a recovered generator.
aesthetics.concept: elegance made measurable — simple, symmetric, resonant structure scores high.

cross_examine.title: Reasonable Doubt
cross_examine.math: an adversarial search for a counter-reading that would defeat the finding.
cross_examine.prog: stress-tests a claim by trying to break it.
cross_examine.concept: a finding is only as strong as the hardest cross-examination it survives.

stare_decisis.title: The Precedent Ledger
stare_decisis.math: a Merkle-chained ledger of prior decisions; departures are flagged against precedent.
stare_decisis.prog: appends each judgment and detects deviations.
stare_decisis.concept: consistency over time — like cases decided alike, and any break made visible.

judgment.title: Earned Finality
judgment.math: a chief-justice rule that grants finality only when doubt has been exhausted.
judgment.prog: combines the doubt search and the precedent ledger into a verdict.
judgment.concept: closure is earned, not assumed — a decision stands only after it has withstood challenge.

candor_bridge.title: Anti-Patronization
candor_bridge.math: scores an explanation across condescension, unearned confidence, evasion, and opacity, tracing each point to its span.
candor_bridge.prog: an audit that returns the offending text, not just a number.
candor_bridge.concept: respect the reader — say what is known plainly and flag what is not.

provenance.title: Conserved History
provenance.math: a conserved-history DAG with minimal-blame attribution (HCT axioms A3/T3).
provenance.prog: tracks where every claim came from and assigns responsibility minimally.
provenance.concept: nothing is unsourced — history is a conserved quantity you can audit back to its origin.

axioms.title: The HCT Axioms
axioms.math: the Holographic Continuity Theory axioms A1–A8 and theorems T1–T6, executed as code.
axioms.prog: each axiom and theorem runs as a checkable unit.
axioms.concept: the theory is not just stated — it is run, so its claims are testable.

infectatrum_bridge.title: The Ambiguity Spectrum
infectatrum_bridge.math: reading-spectrum cardinality |Σ| and Shannon entropy over interpretations, plus an origin-signature (JSD) and an adversarial detector.
infectatrum_bridge.prog: measures how many ways a codified object can be read and how evenly.
infectatrum_bridge.concept: quantify ambiguity and information loss — know exactly how much a surface under-determines its meaning.

uma_bridge.title: The Physical Substrate
uma_bridge.math: tests robustness of recovered structure under the RSLS field dynamics (Lyapunov sensitivity, decoherence).
uma_bridge.prog: perturbs a finding through the substrate and checks it survives.
uma_bridge.concept: a real invariant should be stable under the physics, not an artifact of a quiet input.

vault.title: The One-Command Launcher
vault.math: a small service supervisor — start, wait, signal, reap — over the local processes.
vault.prog: one command starts every service and prints one URL; Ctrl-C stops them all.
vault.concept: the whole system, reachable from a single door.

trace.title: The Reasoning Path
trace.math: exposes the ranked candidate generators, the MDL margin to the runner-up, and the held-out verification.
trace.prog: prints why the winner won, step by step.
trace.concept: the engine shows its work — the decision is legible, not a black box.

compare.title: Versus the Compressors
compare.math: head-to-head against gzip / bz2 / lzma: a constant-size law that regenerates terms general compressors cannot produce.
compare.prog: measures stored size and predictive power side by side.
compare.concept: compression that understands — it stores the rule, not just smaller bytes.

benchmark.title: The Proof
benchmark.math: OEIS-core recovery, classical ciphers, and a randomized adversarial gauntlet scored for false verifications.
benchmark.prog: runs the scored suite and reports the zero that matters.
benchmark.concept: the headline claim, measured: across ~5,070 cases, zero rules certified that it could not predict.

bench_suite.title: The Six Tasks
bench_suite.math: the same architecture across six independent domains versus an established baseline in each.
bench_suite.prog: a consolidated runner with a results table.
bench_suite.concept: one discipline, many fields — each beating or matching a known method, refusing where refusal is right.
